title: Trunk Tools — Deep Research & Competitive Analysis
markdown:
# Trunk Tools — Deep Research & Competitive Analysis

**Research date:** March 18, 2026
**Purpose:** Understand Trunk Tools' offering to position Sagemind competitively for Dempsey Construction

---

## Company Overview

| Detail | Info |
|--------|------|
| **Founded** | ~2021, NYC-based |
| **CEO** | Sarah Buchner |
| **Employees** | ~86 |
| **Revenue** | $8.5M (as of Sept 2025) |
| **Total Funding** | $70M over 3 rounds (Series B: $40M in July 2025, led by Insight Partners) |
| **Other investors** | Redpoint Ventures, Innovation Endeavors, Foundation Capital |
| **Target market** | General contractors, specialty trades |

---

## Products

### 1. TrunkText (Q&A Agent)
- Natural language search across **all project documents**: specs, drawings, schedules, RFIs, submittals, procurement logs, bids, contracts, meeting minutes
- Connects to existing construction software and file storage
- Returns **cited answers** — users can verify the source
- Designed for field workers: reduces document retrieval from "minutes to seconds"
- Field workers spend ~20% of onsite time walking back to trailers for documents — this eliminates that

### 2. TrunkSubmittal (Discrepancy Detection Agent)
- Automatically reviews submittals against project specifications
- Catches **missing, conflicting, and noncompliant information**
- Cross-references trade-related RFIs
- Catches issues before they become schedule delays or costly rework

### 3. TrunkReview (Drawing Revision Agent)
- Scans drawing revisions using **vision language models** trained by architects and construction professionals
- Identifies **both clouded and unclouded changes** (i.e., even changes the architect didn't mark)
- Produces visual overlays and bullet-point change lists with linked sheets
- Processes revisions in minutes
- Claims to prevent "$10,000+ rework events"

### 4. TrunkSOP (Workflow Automation Agent)
- Monitors construction schedules
- Prepares meeting information automatically
- Compares project data against standard operating procedures
- Proactive — identifies risk factors and external dependencies

### Platform-Level Capabilities
- Pre-processes specs, RFIs, submittals, drawings, schedules, contracts, meeting notes, field reports
- Construction-specific AI training (not generic LLM)
- Multi-project support across commercial, residential, and industrial

---

## Integrations

| Platform | Type | Status |
|----------|------|--------|
| **Procore** | Project Management | **⚠️ API ACCESS REVOKED (Sept 2025)** |
| Autodesk Construction Cloud | Project Management | Active |
| SharePoint | File Storage | Active |
| Box | File Storage | Active |
| Dropbox | File Storage | Active |
| Egnyte | File Storage | Active |
| Microsoft Teams | Communication | Active |
| Primavera P6 | Scheduling | Active |

---

## 🚨 CRITICAL: The Procore Ban

### What Happened
In **September 2025**, Procore revoked Trunk Tools' API access. Their booth at Procore's Groundbreak conference (October 2025) was refunded.

### Why
Procore updated its API terms on September 30, 2025 with a new Developer Policy. Key restrictions:
- **Marketplace partners cannot bulk download data for commercial purposes, including training LLMs**
- New "Managed Marketplace" requires comprehensive review for strategic fit, technical feasibility, and security compliance

### The Real Reason (Reading Between the Lines)
Procore launched its own AI features (**Procore Helix** and **Agentic APIs**) — directly competing with what Trunk Tools offers. Industry observers noted that "distribution usually beats innovation" — Procore can replicate startup innovations while leveraging its existing customer base.

### Trunk Tools' Response
CEO Sarah Buchner claimed they applied for marketplace status immediately and made required changes, but were the **only startup not approved**. Their position: "Your data is yours — no third party should limit your access and use."

### What This Means for Us
This is **massive** for our positioning:

1. **Trunk Tools can no longer integrate with Procore** — Dempsey uses Procore
2. Procore's new policy **allows custom integrations** that aren't on the marketplace: *"Third-party integrators will still be able to provide customers with integrations even if not marketed in the Marketplace"*
3. We're building a **custom ChatGPT integration for a specific customer**, not a marketplace product — this likely falls under the custom integration provision
4. We're NOT bulk downloading data for LLM training — we're making **real-time API calls** on behalf of the customer

---

## Procore's Own AI (The New Competitor)

### Procore Helix
- Procore's built-in AI assistant
- Embedded directly in the platform
- General Q&A and document search

### Procore Agentic APIs (Coming Late March 2026)
- New class of APIs built on their "Datagrid" infrastructure
- Deep search across PDFs, images, videos
- Agents that take action across the software stack
- **Consumption-based pricing** (pay per use)
- User/enterprise control, auditing, logging, transparency
- Currently in early access — general availability targeted late March 2026

### Implications
- Procore is trying to own the AI layer for its platform
- But their Agentic APIs are **brand new** and unproven
- Enterprise adoption of Procore\'s own AI will take time
- There's a window of opportunity for custom solutions

---

## Pricing Analysis

### Trunk Tools
- **No public pricing** — requires custom quote
- Industry estimates: **$149/user/month** (basic) up to **$499/user/month** (premium)
- Drew from Dempsey described it as "cost prohibitive"
- With ~20 users at Dempsey, that's **$3,000-$10,000/month** ($36K-$120K/year)

### Sagemind (Our Positioning)
- **$10K one-time** for Phase 1 (ChatGPT + Procore integration)
- No per-user monthly fees
- Customer owns the integration
- Ongoing support/maintenance can be a separate conversation

### Cost Comparison (Illustrative, 20 users)

| | Trunk Tools (est.) | Sagemind | Procore Helix |
|--|-------------------|----------|---------------|
| **Year 1** | $36K-120K | $10K | Included (but limited) |
| **Year 2** | $36K-120K | $0 (owned) | Included |
| **Year 3** | $36K-120K | $0 (owned) | Included |
| **3-Year Total** | $108K-360K | $10K | $0 |
| **Customization** | Limited | Full | None |
| **Procore Integration** | ❌ Revoked | ✅ Custom | ✅ Native |
| **Data Ownership** | Trunk Tools | Customer | Procore |

---

## Competitive Comparison: Feature-by-Feature

### What Trunk Tools Does That We Can Match (Phase 1)

| Feature | Trunk Tools | Sagemind (ChatGPT + Procore) |
|---------|-------------|------------------------------|
| Search RFIs | ✅ TrunkText | ✅ Via Procore API |
| Search Submittals | ✅ TrunkText | ✅ Via Procore API |
| Search Documents | ✅ TrunkText | ✅ Via Procore API |
| Search Drawings | ✅ TrunkText | ⚠️ Metadata yes, PDF content partial |
| Chat interface | ✅ Custom UI | ✅ ChatGPT (familiar to team) |
| Cited sources | ✅ Built-in | ✅ Can return Procore links |
| Mobile access | ✅ Native app | ✅ ChatGPT mobile app |
| Multi-project | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes, via project selection |

### What Trunk Tools Does That We Can Match (Phase 2+)

| Feature | Trunk Tools | Sagemind Roadmap |
|---------|-------------|------------------|
| Submittal review/discrepancy | ✅ TrunkSubmittal | 🔄 Phase 2: Build custom action |
| Drawing revision comparison | ✅ TrunkReview (vision AI) | 🔄 Phase 3: Requires vision model integration |
| Schedule monitoring | ✅ TrunkSOP | 🔄 Phase 2: Via Procore schedule API |
| Workflow automation | ✅ TrunkSOP | 🔄 Phase 2-3: Agentic capabilities |
| Meeting prep automation | ✅ TrunkSOP | 🔄 Phase 2: Automated briefings |

### What We Offer That Trunk Tools Doesn\'t

| Advantage | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **Procore access** | Trunk Tools lost it — we can still build custom integrations |
| **No vendor lock-in** | Customer owns the integration; Trunk Tools is a SaaS dependency |
| **ChatGPT familiarity** | Dempsey already uses ChatGPT; no new UI to learn |
| **One-time cost** | No recurring per-user fees |
| **Customizable** | Built for their specific workflows, not a generic product |
| **Monday.com potential** | We can integrate their CRM/task system too (Trunk Tools doesn\'t) |
| **Physical server potential** | We can explore on-premise integration (Phase 3) |

### What Trunk Tools Has That We Don\'t (Honest Assessment)

| Gap | Impact | Mitigation |
|-----|--------|------------|
| **Purpose-built construction AI** | Their models are trained on construction data | ChatGPT-4 is highly capable at construction terminology with proper prompting |
| **Vision AI for drawings** | TrunkReview uses specialized vision models | Phase 3 feature; GPT-4 Vision can partially address this |
| **Polished UI** | Dedicated construction interface | ChatGPT is familiar and functional; less polished but lower learning curve |
| **Scale/reliability** | $70M funded, 86 employees | We\'re lean but responsive; direct support vs. ticket queue |
| **Multi-platform breadth** | Autodesk, SharePoint, Box, etc. | We start with Procore; expand based on need |

---

## Strategic Recommendations for Dempsey Pitch

### 1. Lead with the Procore Story
Dempsey already evaluated Trunk Tools. They should know:
- Trunk Tools **lost Procore API access** in September 2025
- This means Trunk Tools literally **cannot integrate with Dempsey's primary PM system anymore**
- We build custom integrations that fall under Procore's "custom integration" provision

### 2. Position as the Anti-SaaS
- One-time investment, not a monthly drain
- They own it — no vendor can pull the rug like Procore did to Trunk Tools
- No per-user pricing that scales painfully

### 3. Use the "AI Project Engineer" Vision
- Bryce named this as their dream — use his language
- Phase 1 is the foundation (data access and search)
- Phase 2+ builds toward the agentic project engineer they want
- This is a roadmap, not a one-and-done

### 4. Address the Monday.com Gap
- Drew's biggest pain is cross-system fragmentation (Monday + Procore + CRM)
- Trunk Tools never addressed this — they focus on project documents
- We can pitch a unified AI assistant that spans Procore AND Monday.com

### 5. Security as a Feature
- Unlike Trunk Tools (which got banned partly over data practices), we:
  - Don\'t bulk download or store their data
  - Make real-time API calls with their authorization
  - Give them full control over what's accessible
  - Can provide audit trails

---
